title: CJWasm2 Std Compatibility Improvement Plan

metadata.scope: LLT/API/std L1 compatibility tracked by scripts/std_test.sh

executive_summary.problem: Current std compatibility is limited less by parser basics and more by incomplete standard-library semantic modeling across type inference, CHIR lowering, and runtime/codegen stubs.
executive_summary.primary_driver: The compiler can handle a subset of hard-coded std shapes, but many real std APIs are still missing object models, method signatures, interface semantics, and byte/string/stream behaviors.
executive_summary.strategy: Raise compatibility by first completing console/io object APIs and byte-stream semantics, then closing collection-interface and unicode gaps, and finally tackling binary APIs. Macro-dependent modules remain a separate architecture backlog.
executive_summary.closure_statement: This plan is expected to remove most non-architectural std failures, but it is not by itself a guarantee of 100% std compatibility. A post-A-D reassessment and tail-cleanup phase is required.

current_failure_buckets.notes: Largest failure source; dominated by missing stream objects, byte buffers, readToEnd, and array-byte semantics.
Mostly object-method forms over stdIn/stdErr rather than global print/readln helpers.
Depends on List/Collection/Iterator/Comparable contracts and Ordering behavior.
Currently blocked by missing Rune/String unicode method modeling.
Single bucket but broad API surface; requires endian and bit-level helpers.
Architecture backlog due to macro/annotation dependency; not expected to be fixed by A-D.

explicitly_not_closed_by_A_to_D.reason: Requires macro/annotation architecture, not just std semantic modeling.

known_skip_context.notes: Known skip entries in std_test.sh are not valid short-term closure targets for compatibility percentage.

symptoms: Console.stdIn.read() / readln() object methods not modeled
Console.stdErr.write() / writeln() stream methods not modeled
ByteBuffer, BufferedInputStream, BufferedOutputStream, StringReader, StringWriter APIs lack type and lowering support
Global helpers like readToEnd(...) are not recognized consistently
Built-in interface names exist only as partial whitelists
List<T>/Collection<T>/Iterator<T>/Comparable<T> are not fully treated as implementable semantic contracts
sort-related tests rely on interface inheritance, properties, iterators, and Ordering behavior not fully modeled
Rune unicode case APIs are not modeled
String unicode helpers like toUpper()/toTitle() are not modeled
Bool/Int64 mismatches appear because unicode method return types fall back to incorrect defaults
Array<Byte> and numeric conversion expectations are only partially represented
deriving and argOpt tests depend on macro expansion or custom annotations outside the current architecture

phases.name: Console and Byte-Stream Compatibility
phases.goal: Raise compile pass rate materially by making console/io object APIs compile end-to-end.
success_metrics: Console module compile failures reduced from 5 to <= 1
IO module compile pass count improved beyond 37/90
No regression in cargo test or scripts/system_test.sh

workstreams.title: Introduce pseudo std stream object types
changes: Teach field access inference to recognize Console static fields as built-in stream objects
Assign stable pseudo types for input and output streams
Prevent fallback to Int32/Function for these fields

workstreams.title: Model stream method signatures
changes: Add return types for input/output stream methods
Represent Option-like read APIs correctly where needed
Add compile-safe fallbacks for methods that are only required to compile std tests

workstreams.title: Lower console/io built-ins in CHIR
changes: Add try_lower_builtin_method branches for pseudo stream objects
Map writable stream methods to existing print/eprint runtimes where possible
Introduce zero-value or empty-buffer-safe stubs only where runtime parity is not yet required

workstreams.title: Fix byte-array and readToEnd semantics
changes: Infer readToEnd(...) as Array<UInt8>
Support byte-array equality without incorrect Int32 fallback
Ensure slice/toArray chains over byte buffers preserve byte element types

phases.name: Collection Interface and Sort Semantics
phases.goal: Compile std.sort and collection-style tests that depend on generic interfaces and iterator contracts.
success_metrics: sort module compile failures reduced from 8 to <= 2
List/Collection/Iterator no longer trigger undeclared-type failures in std tests

workstreams.title: Treat built-in collection interfaces as first-class semantic interfaces
changes: Allow struct/class constraint and implementation validation against built-in interfaces
Handle interface property and method completeness checks consistently
Remove undeclared type errors for List/Collection/Iterator in supported std scenarios

workstreams.title: Model Ordering and compare-style APIs
changes: Add Ordering-related return types and compare semantics
Make sort comparator signatures infer correctly
Preserve generic method/property types for custom List implementations

phases.name: Unicode API Coverage
phases.goal: Eliminate unicode module failures caused by missing method metadata and wrong fallback types.
success_metrics: unicode module compile failures reduced from 6 to 0

workstreams.title: Add Rune unicode method support
changes: Model isLowerCase/isUpperCase/isTitleCase as Bool
Model toUpperCase/toTitleCase as Rune
Avoid Bool/Int64 fallback mismatches in unary/binary conditions

workstreams.title: Add String unicode method support
changes: Model toUpper/toTitle/isBlank accurately
Map to available runtime helpers or compile-safe stubs
Verify string-returning methods keep I32 string pointer semantics through lowering

phases.name: Binary API Implementation
phases.goal: Enable compilation of std.binary by implementing the missing endian and bit-level helpers.
success_metrics: binary module compile failures reduced from 1 to 0

workstreams.title: Add endian read/write helpers
changes: Implement readLittleEndian/readBigEndian signatures for integer and float types
Implement writeLittleEndian/writeBigEndian signatures and codegen
Support swapBytes/fromBits helper families needed by std.binary tests

phases.name: Macro-Dependent Modules
phases.goal: Track but do not block on deriving/argOpt until macro support strategy is decided.
success_metrics: Known-skip list remains explicit and accurate

workstreams.title: Keep deriving/argOpt out of short-term compatibility KPIs
changes: Treat macro-based failures as architecture backlog
Expand skip annotations only when the unsupported mechanism is confirmed, not as a workaround for ordinary semantic gaps

phases.name: Post-Fix Reassessment and Tail Cleanup
phases.goal: Re-run std compatibility after A-D, re-cluster any residual non-macro failures, and create the final closure pass.
success_metrics: Residual non-macro failures are enumerated with concrete root causes
A-D hidden follow-on failures are converted into explicit work items
Remaining failures after excluding macro/known-skip items are small and localized

workstreams.title: Re-baseline std compatibility after Phases A-D
changes: Run std_test again and record the new failure set
Separate architecture-blocked failures from ordinary semantic/runtime gaps
Produce a residual-failure report by module and error signature

workstreams.title: Create the final tail-fix batch
changes: Append remaining non-macro issues as concrete tasks with file ownership and validation commands
Use this phase to close any issues revealed only after the major API surfaces are implemented

what_A_to_D_should_achieve: Resolve the majority of current compile failures in non-macro std modules
Substantially raise the default L1 std compile pass rate from the current 52%
Remove the largest structural blockers in io, console, sort, unicode, and binary
what_A_to_D_will_not_guarantee: Full closure of macro/annotation dependent modules
100% std compatibility without a residual-tail cleanup pass
Perfect runtime fidelity for every newly compiling std test on the first pass
delivery_expectations.closure_condition: The plan should be considered complete only after Phase F confirms that all remaining non-skip failures are either resolved or explicitly reclassified as architecture backlog.

notes: This plan is for std compatibility only and does not replace the broader conformance negative-test roadmap.
Compile-rate wins should come before runtime-perfect fidelity in std_test, but system_test.sh must remain green.
Avoid broad fallback-to-Int32 behavior in any new std modeling; every new API should carry an explicit type.
Phases A-D are intentionally scoped to the 73 currently identified non-macro failures, not to the full 88-failure set.
Any claim that the plan can fix everything would be inaccurate until Phase F residual reassessment is done.
